meaning: n.圆凿,沟,以圆凿刨vt.用圆凿子削除,欺骗,挖出
examples: In the Middle Ages, a favourite punishment was to gouge out a prisoner's eyes.
You can blind or gouge him if you anticipate the third seduce.
Bandage very early, as soon as you lose 1250 health, gouge, jump away, bandage.
Your critics say you used the power you achieved in the high-yield market to gouge people.
There is some evidence that banks have recently been trying to gouge better prices from worse borrowers.
